title: Fix broken login authentication flow

requirements.text: Audit and fix `frontend/src/lib/auth.ts` so that the `login` function correctly retrieves the stored credential for a given username from `globalchat_users` in localStorage and compares it against the provided password using the exact same encoding scheme (base64 `btoa`) used by the `register` function. Fix any mismatch between how passwords are encoded on registration versus how they are compared on login. The `seedOwnerAccount` function must seed the `AI.Caffeine` account with `btoa('2580')` in a way that is consistent with the login comparison. Wrap all localStorage reads and writes in try/catch blocks so no exception propagates to the render tree.
acceptanceCriteria: A user who registers with username 'testuser' and password 'abc123' can successfully log in with those exact credentials on the next attempt.
The `AI.Caffeine` account can be logged into with password '2580'.
Logging in with a wrong password shows an inline error message such as 'Invalid username or password.' rather than a blank screen or unhandled error.
All localStorage operations in auth.ts are wrapped in try/catch and never throw uncaught exceptions.
The encoding scheme used in `register`, `login`, and `seedOwnerAccount` is identical (all use `btoa` for encoding and `atob`-based comparison for verification).

requirements.text: Audit and fix `frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx` to ensure the login form correctly calls the `login` function from `frontend/src/lib/auth.ts`, displays a clear inline error message ('Invalid username or password.') on failure, and on success writes the authenticated session to `globalchat_session` in localStorage (with fields `username` and `isAuthenticated: true`) before navigating to ChatPage. The Create Account flow must call `register` and then immediately log the user in and navigate to ChatPage. Wrap all localStorage operations in try/catch.
acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the login form with valid credentials navigates the user to ChatPage without a blank screen.
Submitting the login form with invalid credentials shows 'Invalid username or password.' inline, not a generic 'failed' message.
Creating a new account and submitting automatically logs the user in and navigates to ChatPage.
After successful login, `globalchat_session` in localStorage contains `{ username: '...', isAuthenticated: true }`.
No uncaught errors are thrown during the login or registration flows.

requirements.text: Audit `frontend/src/App.tsx` to ensure that after `LandingPage` sets `globalchat_session` in localStorage, the app immediately re-renders and shows `ChatPage`. The synchronous lazy `useState` initializer must detect a valid authenticated session (`isAuthenticated: true`) on mount and render `ChatPage` directly. Additionally, expose a stable `onLogin` or `setSession` callback to `LandingPage` so that when the user logs in, the React state in `App.tsx` is updated synchronously — not relying solely on a page reload or re-mount to pick up the new session.
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful login in LandingPage, the app transitions to ChatPage without requiring a full page reload.
Opening the app in a new tab while already logged in (valid `globalchat_session` in localStorage) immediately shows ChatPage, not a blank screen.
Opening the app with no session shows LandingPage immediately.
The app never renders a blank screen after the initial synchronous render cycle.

constraints: Do not change `frontend/src/main.tsx` or any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
Do not alter the backend schema or Motoko actor.
The AI.Caffeine account password must remain '2580' and the owner account seeding must not overwrite other user accounts.
nonGoals: Adding new features or admin commands.
Changing the visual design or layout.
Fixing any issues unrelated to the login/authentication flow.
